title: SOC 2 CC7 — Vulnerability Management SLA and Exception Expiry Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `index=vuln` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:vuln","qualys:host_vm_detection")` (plugin_id, severity, first_found, patch_due_date, host_fqdn); `vuln_exceptions.csv` (plugin_id, host_fqdn, expires_on, approver)
app: Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060), Qualys Technology Add-on (Splunkbase 2964)
spl:
index=vuln sourcetype="tenable:vuln" severity IN ("Critical","High") earliest=-1d
| eval due=strptime(patch_due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnull(due) OR due<now()
| lookup vuln_exceptions.csv plugin_id host_fqdn OUTPUT expires_on
| eval exp=strptime(expires_on,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnull(exp) OR exp<now()
| stats dc(host_fqdn) as hosts, values(plugin_id) as plugins by severity
| sort severity
value: CC7 expects vulnerability management. Splunk consolidates scanner output with SLAs and risk-acceptance expirations so overdue criticals and stale exceptions cannot hide in siloed tools—direct evidence for patch-management controls.
implementation: (1) Normalize severity strings; (2) integrate exception workflow approvals; (3) auto-expire exceptions in GRC; (4) map hosts to owners via CMDB; (5) weekly leadership metric: overdue critical count.
visualization: Table (overdue), Single value (host count), Treemap by business unit from CMDB join.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
